reviews.review_description:
First of all, thanks to the YouTube channel @vraimentcoolofficiel and their contest for giving me the opportunity to play this game by winning a key.

Looking at the video it seems cool but in reality you don't get to do anything... The game consists of 21 MR missions + 8 for "large spaces". However, although I've scanned other rooms, everything's going in the same room where I play. I play in a room of 30m² with furniture.

The missions can go from 10" up to, let's say 2' which is not at all engaging. The replayability relies on the fact that you need a perfect score but it doesn't make me want to replay the missions. 

In every mission, the objective is the same: hack a computer and retrieve intel needing whether to neutralize the enemies whether to avoid lasers (or both). No real stealth, no puzzles, you just go with the tranquillizing darts and you use your weapon if there are robots. I've got to use the voice commands one time only. The physics are like Espire 2 (not really developed) and the MR is not really optimal with some glitches (sometimes ennemies disappear but we can see them in Espire vision).

Although I like the Espire series game style, I cannot put a higher score for the moment as it doesn't meet neither the quantity nor the quality. I hope that we will see improvements in the future to a Hitman-like or escape room-like game.
One of my favorite mixed reality experiences - and it has Quest Achievements!

For a mr game based on one of the best vr single player co-op games this is one of the best mr games ive played Espire is one of my favorite vr series and seeing the game as a full mr game is truly a fun experience though on Espire 2 even though its on a cliff hanger i wonder if the fans will get a third episode or game

Possibly one of the most impressive use of MR I've seen to date.

The game is broken down into small bitesized mission that play out in your scanned play space.

There's something about physically go crawling on the floor to go under a laser or standing perfectly still as motion sensors pass over you

Or seeing enemies bursting through a vent in your living room ceiling and rappelling down to kill you....that just sparks that inner child within me.

Levels have some repeatability in form of unlocking "cheats" to mess around with but otherwise its a pretty short experience (that will just take a couple of hours to get through)

But if you have the required playspace needed for the game....its simply one a must have MR experience both for your self and to show to non VR playing friends/family

And as an EA experience there is possibly more to come in the future!!

Warning - I believe the MR missions are included in Espire 2 so its a cheap way for you to experience just the MR element of the game.
So owners of Espire 2 may want to just check it out in that.

There's also a significant amount of space needed to play so keep that in mind.

developer_response.body:
Hey BurntBreadman, thanks for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. We're so stoked you've been enjoying the game so far! We really hope people enjoy showcasing Mixed Reality to friends and family with Espire - that's one of our big dreams for this game. 

Our current primary focus is in improving our game's level initialization tech so that more of our game's existing levels + future levels, will be able to work in smaller and smaller rooms, or rooms with all different types of furniture. It will be an iterative process, but expect to see improvements with each update. 

We hope the game will continue to entertain as it evolves. Thanks again for leaving your review!

Cheers, EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description:
I initially bounced off the MR missions as my house just wasn’t the correct shape for them to work properly, though I could see the potential. After the latest update I can enjoy a lot more of the game, and at the current price I’d say it was a steal!

Both the main Espire games are well worth the purchase too and I highly recommend them, I’ve had many, many hours of fun in both games.

Espire 1 and 2 were already ahead of their time, and Lode Digital once again proves why they are true pioneers in VR. As early as 2022, the studio was experimenting with advanced Mixed Reality concepts that are only now becoming mainstream. Espire: MR Missions builds on that foundation brilliantly.

This experience features redesigned Mixed Reality missions inspired by Espire 2, resulting in 29 unique missions (21 small-scale and 8 large-scale). Stepping into the role of a secret stealth agent feels fantastic—think 007 in VR, but in your own physical space. The stealth mechanics are incredibly well crafted, the pacing is excellent, and the execution is rock solid.

It’s one of those experiences I love showcasing to friends, just to watch their reactions as they realize how naturally MR blends with stealth gameplay. Espire: MR Missions is a must-have and easily earns a permanent spot in any VR library.

developer_response.body:
metaquesta99, we really appreciate your review. Thanks for taking the time to post it.   We're really glad you've been enjoying the game. We've also seen your gameplay streams shared on social media. Stoked to see the game being enjoyed! We hope it will continue to entertain as it continues to evolve with improved features & new content. Thanks for supporting us with Espire 1, 2 and now Espire: MR Missions. 

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: This MR experience gave me a new respect for game development. When something feels effortless to the player, especially in mixed reality it usually means a lot of invisible work behind the scenes. As someone building in VR/MR, that effort doesn’t go unnoticed. Also appreciated as an owner of the previous title I was able to get this port for free. Keep rocking my people.

You are tasked with taking on missions, small and large, the game loads the missions into your scanned play area, which consist of stealth based gameplay, sneaking up on the targets unseen and unheard is key to success..
 I liked the use of the voice commands in game and the xray vision, graphically impressive aswell very cool mixed reality experience highly recommended if you like stealth and mixed reality games

developer_response.body:
Hey Anthony9021, thanks for your review and for playing our game. We're stoked you've enjoyed it and we are working hard to ensure it will continue to entertain as the game grows. 

Cheers! 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description:
This game is best described as playing mission impossible in your living room.  Ducking lasers between my couch, go up to the enemy that just stands next to my own wall, silent take down, then take his weapon and unleash hell through a virtual window.  There are a bunch of these scenarios.  This game is the absolute peak of Mix Virtual Reality in the current generation.
This game makes me wish that I have a much bigger house.  I love that this is a perfect addition to the excellent Espire series.

developer_response.body:
Snorlax.GMax, we greatly appreciate your review.  Thanks for playing Espire: MR Missions. We will continue to evolve this game with new content, and with a goal for our existing content to support smaller rooms and rooms with interesting furniture layouts, which sometimes block our current system from supporting some levels. We hope the game continues to entertain!

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: This is likely going to become my new “go to” for friends new to VR so that they can get the hang of things before jumping into a full VR experience, and with this price, it’s well worth it! The mechanics are great, the voiceover is very helpful for immersion, and overall I had a great time with this game

Holy smokes - you better be ready for a very challenging experience. This game has an incredible learning curve. And be ready to avoid lasers that will kill you on the spot. I’ve been playing this game and still haven’t figured it all out yet, but I think it’s great .

reviews.review_title: A  friendly game to new people to VR
reviews.review_description:
Espire is great Mxr game due to using your whole room to use to sneak around and stealthily take out the enemies for the bigger levels of the game, the smaller levels in the game have a windows leading into the enemies base but you can only shoot to other side unless you want to break a wall by trying to get to the other side the game does re use the first couple levels from Espire 2 in this game but expands on the later missions, i would recommend this to anyone who loves stealth or just wants to cause chaos by going in loud and I would recommend especially for anyone new to vr because of how easy it is to get used to moving around a room in real life and using cool gadgets and weapons virtually.

I do like the concept of turning a mode from Espire 2 into its own game and would love to see what's next for this game, like more weapons, new levels or even game modes like the training levels for weapons but its like a kill house and have to run around your house and take out the decoys, Or a way to make this multiplayer would be interesting to see to play locally and fight together or fight against each other.

reviews.review_title: A stand out mixed reality experience
reviews.review_description:
As a big fan of Espire 2, this has come as a great bonus, I just need a bigger lounge, I need a battleground to really get the full experience.
Graphically is so good and the immersion is bang on.
Great tutorial to get started and superb action once you get going.
This is an amazing add on to the Espire collection, really enjoyed what I have played so far.

I haven't been too high on a lot of MR so far, but Espire blew away my expectations.  I've very cool to actually move around your environment and interact with in-game objects.  The enemies are fun to encounter, and it's something special to hide in a virtual locker in your real space.  What an experience!

reviews.review_title: Playing spy in your own home
reviews.review_description: As a concept i really like it. You get to play a robot spy in your room with small physics obstacles and enemies you can fight in but often outside your room. It shines when the game pressures you with multitasking dodging lasers while taking out soldiers, but it seems only truly worth it if you have enough space. It is fun, but the missions for small rooms are just too short. When it starts to get going its already over and going through menus and loading screens takes almost as much time as gameplay (in smaller rooms) itself.

reviews.review_title: Fun MR Title With Variety Mission Types
reviews.review_description: Espire MR mission has similar mechanics to the full game but focuses on the mixed reality aspects. The missions can be challenging to get a decent score but that is what makes you want to keep coming back for more. There are several mission types with hacking, stealth, combat and obstacles / lasers to avoid. Overall it's a fun little game that could be even better with a little more variation between the missions.

I’ve never been the biggest fan of MR games but this game may have changed my mind it felt like I was really in those missions as it has you hiding behind furniture and making you question you’re next move there are also tons of missions allowing you to never get bored and when you do beat them all you can go back and get a better time

Having a slight preference to MR over VR for games that require a lot of movement, I was excited to give this a go. It was such a great time to crawl about my own home, completing tasks and taking out the enemy. This gane took me thriugh various rooms of my house as well as outside! The tasks are quick and you can continue to build skills. I really like that it changes the layout so you're constantly challenged when restarting. Enjoyed applying some MMA to the uninvited goons. Gave 4 out of 5 because the outside experience lacked the passthrough I needed to keep myself safe while immersed.

Full disclosure: a copy of the game was provided. 

￼Espire MR provides players with exciting tactical action experiences in their own living room, or office space, or any area that they room scan to use. Recent updates have evolved this application into a fun (previously flawed, scanning and room-size issues have been addressed) experience, one that those seeking to find out for themselves the potential for mixed reality on headsets should definitely check out. The price point is more than fair. 

Fans of games like metal gear solid will enjoy finding themselves in the middle of action, or stealth for the missions requiring avoiding lasers and other such hazards. The game has enough variety in its tactics that it does not feel repetitive. The only criticism I can recall is that the scoring system could be better explained so that players understood what was needed in order to achieve a higher score on a specific level. It feels like players are being timed to complete actions quickly, which can always be slightly hazardous whenever moving around a physical space. ￼￼￼

Great Augmented reality game. I playtested the game through impact labs and it was a great experience, even in my tiny apartment full of furniture the game was still able to create fun and engaging levels, so if your worried you don't have the space you don't need to. Lots of mission variety and the score system will have you trying to be more efficient each time you replay.

Espire MR is an interesting game, it tries to both turn your room into a sort of spy obstacle course full of lasers and traps, and expand upon it with virtual windows and hatches. Peering through a virtual window to see a full sized military base or seeing soldiers break into your room through a hole in the ceiling makes for a uniquely immersive feeling when it's happening in your actual surroundings. When it works, it's a lot of fun, but I would often run into near game-breaking bugs.
Level objectives would often be placed inside of furniture, especially my bed. My room is a little cluttered for this kind of thing, but I have plenty of open spaces that the game could be using but would often choose not to. I also ran into a few instances where I would be required to eliminate guards, but they would stand behind tanks or virtual crates where I couldn't reach them and refuse to move, not reacting to the gunfire around them at all.
The issues are not constant, however, and would usually be resolved by restarting the game. I had more fun than frustration with this game, but when the room tracking or the enemy AI would break, it got pretty annoying and there were multiple occasions where I just stopped playing the game after realizing halfway through a mission that something I needed to interact with was inside of my mattress again.
Overall, it's a fun time, but I hope further patches will improve the object placement algorithm and the enemy AI.

Definitely a great MR game, for those that want a largescale MR mission inspired game it's great you just need quite a bit of space so for those that have small rooms this definitely isn't for you even though some work for small rooms. There are some missions that are similar and the mechanics are truly great, definitely worth trying!

Espire: MR Missions is a slick, confidence boosting way to feel like a stealth operative in your own living room. The mixed reality setup is the star here, turning your real space into a tactical playground where peeking around actual corners and using your room layout adds a fun layer of immersion you just do not get in standard VR. A big plus is that it supports play across room sizes, so you are not locked out if you do not have a huge open area. The missions are bite sized and satisfying, with that “one more run” rhythm that makes it easy to jump in, feel cool, and hop back out. That said, the experience can be a little picky about room setup, and I hit an occasional message saying a mission was not compatible with my current space, which can break the flow. When everything clicks, though, it is tense, polished, and genuinely unique for MR on Quest, making it an easy recommendation if you want stealth action with a clever real world twist.

It's a very solid mixed reality experience, and you do need a sizeable room for some of the missions. I played in my living room, it was overall an enjoyable experience. However, occasionally, some mission objectives would spawn within a wall ever so slightly, but I assume it was just a scanning issue.

reviews.review_title: Great MR experience with bugs to workout
reviews.review_description:
This is the MR game that everyone needs to show off just what building a gaming experience in your own home should look like. However, the version I was given to test just had too many bugs to fully enjoy it. Room scanning and weapon handling need to be ironed out. I was unable to play indoors after the patch was released, so I'll comment about playing in my backyard.
The feeling of actually being in a clip of a "movie" where you have to use the limited tools and spy gadgets is quite immersive. Ducking behind VR cabinets and standing next to doors, awaiting an enemy to come through so you can knock them out, so much fun. The levels are segmented out so you can get an idea of each type of experience, be out shooting, stealth, or even using voice commands to interrogate. There is quite a bit of fun to be had in the somewhat limited scope there is here.
I am fully inclined to play this when it does work properly, and I felt that I should share the good and only briefly give grievances since the developers have worked up an exceptional MR experience. Give me a campaign that works, and I'll be back with a 5 to gladly hand out.

reviews.review_title: First MR Game I’ve Actually Enjoyed
reviews.review_description: Wasn’t expecting much, but I truly enjoyed this game. It’s not perfect but definitely has potential. I overly enjoyed the TRANQ lol probably my favorite weapon so far. The voice commands were AWESOME. Overall the game needs a bit of polish but is a great experience so far.

developer_response.body:
Thanks JustinKase.exe :) Glad you found this to be an enjoyable MR experience - we take that as a huge compliment. We're stoked to hear you like the arm-mounted tranq launcher too - we can understand when players say the opposite - that they'd prefer it was a pistol, but we did want to try something 'inbuilt' into the Espire frame. 

Regarding polish, it is our primary focus at the moment, particularly on improving how our game initializes its mission within player's rooms. We want it to continually support smaller and smaller spaces. 

Thanks for taking the time to post your review. We're working hard to hopefully one day turn this into a 5 star review when the game warrants it. 

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_title: Fun and easy to pick up!
reviews.review_description: I had a lot of fun playing espire mr! It was super fluent with a lot of controls and had that sort of feel from the original espire 1 game. I’d revoke her this to a lot of people who want to have some fun quick either on the go or at home!

This game turns your living room into a full blown action set piece, delivering one of the most impressive mixed reality experiences available. Missions play out directly in your scanned space, with lasers, enemies, and gadgets interacting naturally with your real walls, floors, and ceilings.

Crawling under lasers, freezing as sensors pass over you, or watching enemies drop in through your ceiling is thrilling and incredibly immersive. The visuals are sharp, the tutorial is well paced, and the action quickly ramps up.

While the experience is fairly short and benefits from a larger play space, the creativity and immersion more than make up for it. This is a must have mixed reality showcase and a fantastic addition to the Espire universe.

Espire: MR Missions is one of the few mixed reality games that truly feels designed around MR rather than using it as a gimmick. Your real room becomes the level, and that fundamentally changes how you play. Using your own walls and furniture for cover, crouching under lasers stretched across your floor, and watching enemies enter through virtual doors cut into your walls creates moments that feel genuinely tense and memorable.

The stealth gameplay works especially well in this format. Peeking around real corners, freezing as motion sensors sweep past you, or quietly taking down an enemy standing next to your couch feels surprisingly immersive. Voice commands and spy tools like Espire Vision add another layer, making you feel like an actual operative instead of just someone wearing a headset.

The missions are short and bite sized, but that feels intentional. It’s easy to jump in, run a few missions, and walk away feeling like you pulled off something cool. They’re clearly built for replay, encouraging you to improve your score, move more efficiently, and experiment with different approaches. While the overall experience isn’t very long at the moment, it never feels padded or dragged out.

Being an Early Access title, there are still some rough edges. Room scanning and object placement can occasionally be inconsistent, and having a larger, cleaner play space definitely improves the experience. That said, when everything clicks, Espire: MR Missions delivers something truly unique.

This isn’t just a fun side project or add-on to the Espire series it’s one of the strongest examples so far of what mixed reality on Meta Quest can be when it’s taken seriously.
It’s so good, I have to explain just get the game

Espire: MR Missions is the standalone version of Espire 2 MR mode.
The studio made this difficult choice in order to start from scratch (Espire 1 & 2 being full VR games), integrate the latest advancements in MR technology, and improve and add new content.

Like any MR game, there shouldn't normally be any motion sickness since the game will require you to move in your playing area. There are various accessibility options regarding the gameplay itself.

MR modes in VR are rarely very ambitious and often resemble a gimmick mode most of the time.
This is not the case here: the game currently consists of 29 missions (21 that require a small environment, 8 that require a larger area : including 4 that require a more wider).
The missions are perfectly calibrated for short sessions, with an average duration of approximately 1 to 2 minutes if successful.
And each one is varied in its approach and fills the agenda of the perfect spy: infiltration, laser dodging, puzzle-solving, hacking, shooting sessions, etc...
But if you like to demonstrate that you are the best of the best Espire agents, each mission has 2 leaderboards (one in terms of speed and the other based on efficiency) which greatly extends the playtime length.

The negative points : the space required to play properly (which is close to that of roomscale games), the surface scanning which I find less effective than other competitors, and the content available to date (in a straight line) which will occupy you less than 3 hours.

Keep in mind that technical problems can be fixed and will surely be improved as well: the game is in Early Access.
And having played it beforehand, a lot of things have already been improved : such as a complete translation, some missions tweaked to be simpler or more understandable, written and video tutorials added, weapon handling improved, etc...
Even if it's not perfect, the qualities outweigh the few flaws: it's simply one of the best MR game available.

Espire 3 when? <3
Gut

reviews.review_title: Mixed feelings on this Mixed Reality
reviews.review_description:
The general idea of these missions is incredible and it really is an enjoyable immersive experience when it works.  I even had a mission I'll probably never forget where an enemy walked into an "elevator" and I punched him to knock him out...only that elevator was my closet door and I full-punched it then was confused why I couldn't reach into my door to pick him up.  Moments like that make me want to rate this 5-stars.  Also, huge shout-out to the team for making cheats that don't just make the game easier.  However, there are two big issues that make me dock a star each (which matches my "Meh" experience).  

The first big issue was getting room scanning to work with the game.  The game doesn't yet use Quest's scan fully and I think that's the primary issue for me.  The main room I have was fine but every one of the rooms were either gone or shifted to the point of being unplayable and the game refused to use those rooms.  Not only that, but if you properly label your furniture as "couch" and so on, the game does annoyingly bad things like put items literally inside the back of the couch.  I'm also pretty sure (because I've never seen this before) the game ruined my room scans on multiple occasions, shifting them slightly over or turning them.  I've re-scanned my entire house FOUR times and it still doesn't really work.

The other big issue is how the missions are designed in my house and scoring.  I've never had a laser mission where I actually had to dodge the lasers unless it's the spinning one that takes up the entire room.  Windows spawn in horrible spots where they're blocked off by real world items (even with the update).  Scoring feels sporadic where I'll get good ranks for bad attempts or never reach 5-star (like pistol training) despite feeling like I easily did top 20% performance.  I could go on, but Meta limits review length.

I recieved a free copy for testing, but I do think this is worth the cost if you're okay with all this.

developer_response.body:
Hey AoraMew. Thanks for taking the time to leave such an in-depth review. Sorry you've experienced these issues related to the technical side of the game & with the Meta room scanning. It's the main reason we've launched into early access - continually improving the room generation for our game, and working with Meta to fix room scan issues. At the time of writing, most room scan issues are fixed by resetting the headset and launching the game again but we're aware of just how frustrating that can be. 

As more players try our game and provide feedback, we're able to better improve our level initialization tech so the levels play better and better in rooms of all sizes. There's even optional analytics that players can choose to provide where we can use the room mesh data to troubleshoot issues. So expect to see better results with future updates. 

Thankyou again for your feedback and for trying our game! Cheers, 

EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: Played the original and always wanted more but also thought that the mixed reality feature on quest was severely underused by games and this game is a dream I recommend this to anyone who likes stealth and mixed reality

This is a fun MR game where windows are placed around your playspace shoot at enemies.  Interactive items such as levers, laptops, buttons are also placed around you must use in order to complete missions.  There's a variety of missions meant for small or larger playspaces.  It doesn't take too long to complete the game but I have found a lot of reeplayability in trying to earn higher stars for faster completion of missions.  I did have problems with my playspace at times.  The game would put one of the interactive items inside a piece of my furniture or place the start up box you stand in to start the mission inside a chair.  I would restart the mission and get a different start up box and item placement and be able to continue playing.  Oh, you can also yell commands at enemies and they react to it.  I've had a lot of fun with these MR missions.

developer_response.body:
Potatochip007 - we love your gamertag. Thankyou for leaving such an in-depth review. We're glad that overall you've been enjoying the game!

Reading that sometimes, items are spawning within furniture, I hope to offer a tip: Check to see if your Meta scan has included that piece of furniture as part of the scan. 

After doing a room scan, Meta's OS will create virtual 'objects' for all your furniture. If there isn't a virtual object for a piece of furniture, our game won't know it exists. For example, if you have a chrome fridge, or a glass display cabinet, sometimes the Meta scan wont create virtual objects for that furniture, because the reflective chrome / glass materials aren't scanned correctly. So you will have to 'draw object' yourself. 

if you have drawn the object and our game is still placing it's objective items inside your furniture, then that sounds like a bug. We're always trying to find and fix bugs like that, and so hopefully we can address any bugs like that in our upcoming updates. 

Thanks again for your review!

Cheers,
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description:
This mixed reality game delivers an incredibly immersive experience by turning your own home—or even an outdoor space—into the battlefield. Whether you’re playing in a small room or a wide-open area, the game adapts seamlessly. After scanning your environment, it intelligently uses your real-world layout and adds mission-specific elements directly into your space, making every session feel personal and dynamic.

Missions are fast-paced and rewarding, encouraging you to improve your performance. The quicker you complete each round, the more stars you earn. Playing on the Quest 3, I experienced zero motion sickness, which makes this an excellent choice for beginners. As you progress, the game gradually introduces new abilities, keeping the gameplay fresh and motivating without ever feeling overwhelming.

The realism and immersion are standout features. The mixed reality elements blend so naturally with your surroundings that it’s easy to forget you’re playing a game. A word of caution, though—especially when playing outside—make sure your play area is flat and obstacle-free. 

Combat is where the game truly shines. The AI enemies look realistic and react to your voice, making encounters feel intense and unpredictable. Enemies can rappel through your ceiling or emerge from vents in your space, which is both thrilling and slightly nerve-wracking. Tools like x-ray vision add a strategic layer, helping you track threats and plan your approach. 

Using a virtual window to locate enemies, you can choose to shoot, stun, or sneak up on them, giving you multiple ways to complete each mission. The mix of stealth, action, and mixed reality interaction makes every encounter engaging. 

Overall, this game is a highly immersive, beginner-friendly mixed reality experience that feels innovative, intense, and incredibly fun—just make sure to play it somewhere safe, because it’s easy to forget the real world entirely.

reviews.review_title: MR in your own living room
reviews.review_description:
Your living room is transformed into a battlefield.

When you run out of ammo, search your surroundings for it.

Try to complete each round as quickly as possible to get the most ⭐️. I repeated some rounds multiple times to get a better score, which wasn't always successful.

It was a bit of a shock when an armed man suddenly appeared in my living room. He had emerged from a ventilation shaft.
So when you start a round, look carefully around to see where enemies are emerging...

The game environment is very realistic.

reviews.review_title: Good MR experience, needs some polish
reviews.review_description:
I think if you're looking for novel MR experiences, this game is for you!

Espire MR Missions turns your home space into a variety of espionage obstacle courses and stealth/action sequences. There are a variety of interesting mechanics, such as "espire vision" (a cool looking luminance-inverted and colorized x-ray spy vision that outlines enemies, electrical conduits, etc) and limited-time slow motion, as well as weapons like tranquilizer darts, shotguns, rifles, etc. You can also speak and use voice commands to hold up enemies at gun-point. When a mission starts, it'll populate your physical space with lasers to dodge, laptops to hack, ammo crates to use as cover, etc. Windows and door will open up in your wall to reveal virtual spaces filled with enemies and objectives.

There are missions for small spaces, and a handful of missions for larger rooms and multi-room setups. When I was initially playtesting, a number of the small space missions gave me an error code saying my space was too small, but that appears to have been fixed for the most part, as I only had that with one single small-room mission when I played the release build. In the release build, I managed to play most of the large-room missions - a few of them felt similar to their small-room counterparts, but some really expanded the experience. The large-room missions say they *can* potentially even work across multiple rooms simultaneously, but the only way to do this appears to be to force meta's "space setup" to treat multiple rooms as one single space - which it does not want to do (I had to force my headset to keep scanning past the point that it said it was done).

My major gripe is that sometimes the virtual object placement isn't great. I've had virtual windows and doors I'm supposed to look through end up spawning in inconvenient and awkard spaces, and I've had interaction objects (levers to pull, etc) spawn in really not ideal places.

I'm giving Espire MR Missions a 3/5, but if they fix some of the aforementioned spawning issues that can sometimes appear, I think this could be a 4/5 for me. I recommend the devs enable players to identify which walls are most ideal for virtual windows/doors/etc, and which to avoid. It's clear they're still really working hard to improve the game, so I have faith they will!

I got *zero* motion sickness from this game, which is fantastic! I had fun treating the lasers/hacking/etc as a sort of physical role-playing.

I *did* get this game for free as a playtester, though I feel that the price is reasonable for the amount of content. If you rush through each mission without any fail-states, it's likely 1.5 hours of content roughly - but obviously the missions can spawn differently each time you play them, especially across different spaces, so there is replay value.

reviews.review_title: Bring the fight to your living room
reviews.review_description:
Bring the fight to your living room - literary

Espire MR missions brings the fight into your home with realistic looking enemy AI that can actually respond to your voice commands.  The combat in many missions is through a large window drawn into your wall but dont be surprised if a hatch opens in your ceiling and a life size soldier repels down to try to shoot you.  The game uses space setup to place objects in your home i found that the more open space you have the better your experience will be.  My current space is not large enough to do any of the large missions. The guns and weapons felt great

reviews.review_title: I want to like this, I do...
reviews.review_description:
But it just doesn't seem to work all that great so far. I had a really hard time making it work with my play space. I went around and scanned the entirety of my first floor to have a lot of moving space and it just wouldn't really use the space properly. Spawns were being placed inside walls or the spawn was right next to the terminal and sometimes I could simply just bypass any challenge that was set up. 

I redid my room scan several times but it was just one issue after the next until I gave up. 

The visuals are nice though and the use of mixed reality is cool, when there's a window through a wall and stuff. Hoping I can play this more easily in the future.

developer_response.body:
Hey M9playsgames. Thanks for taking the time to leave your review, and sorry that you've experienced these issues with the game. We are aware there's issues to fix both within our game, and also on the Meta scanning side. At the time of writing, I can say that most room scan issues like the ones you describe can be fixed by restarting your headset. We are fully aware of how much friction that adds. 

For example - if you adjust your scan, sometimes the revised scan from Meta isn't actually provided to the game, even after restarting the game. The whole headset needs to be turned on and off again and then it works. It's one of 4 major issues we're working with Meta to get fixed in a future OS update. 

On our side, we're continuing to improve the way our game initializes its missions within player's spaces, and also allowing existing and future missions to work in smaller and smaller rooms, or rooms with limited floor and wall space. So we hope in the future that this game , and the Meta OS, can provide the experience you hoped for.  

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_title: Expand your room into action spaces
reviews.review_description: Pretty advanced MR gaming offer: the game uses real world data of your (multi)rooms to expand it with virtual stealth action through simulated windows and entry doors. Great variety of short missions ranging from clearing guarded spaces and fighting intruders to exploration with drones and avoiding laser traps. Especially impressive with voice control of responsive NPCs! Easy to set up, but hard to master. Most complete and convincing MR game I played up to now.

- Disclaimer: Received a playtest copy for free - I'm always looking for new and interesting mixed reality games to play. Mixed reality can be hit-or-miss, but Espire: MR Missions is one of the best at demonstrating what can be accomplished when a dev puts effort into really making it work. The have simple elements, but are put together into a compelling way that makes you want to replay them to get a better score. My one issue is that *sometimes* the mission elements will exist outside the playspace, but the developer has put out updates to address this. Highly recommended if you're looking for a great MR game to add to your library.

Really well done, tbh I wasn't expecting much in the beginning think that it's just another one of those simple run and gun shooter with added mixed reality. But when i learned that there's voice command, especially when it works really well I know I have to give it more time and give it another chance. The x-ray vision is so much fun too and unexpected, recommend for everyone, super beginner friendly too so if u have motion sickness, still give it a try.

This is a game in which espire2's extended MR mission pack was separated and more new missions were added. It uses perfect space data including furniture composition and multiple rooms, so playing in a spacious and complex space adds fun. It creates a virtual environment by utilizing elements such as walls and door window ceilings quite well, turning my living room into a tense battlefield. The main purpose is infiltrating spies, so it's a challenge that best fits MR than the other MR games such as simple shooting or horde mode battle. Even in the same environment, the deployments are newly positioned at the start of each mission, so you can feel different even if I repeat it. As I proceed with the missions one by one, the abilities to be used increases, so I liked to continuously play missions once started.

reviews.review_title: One of the best MR experiences
reviews.review_description: Espire VR is a very impressive MR game that brings the action into your home.  It's very fun to look out a virtual window and shoot targets, or sneak up and stop invading enemies.  I am very impressed on how well this works with various room size setups. I tried both small and large missions and they worked amazing.  Lots of fun different mission types, avoiding the lasers is fun and I do like how I didn't have to physically crawl on the ground, it had a nice medium of movement and interaction.  The missions are broken up into small portions so it makes it nice if you just want to put on the headset and play a few quick missions. I would definitely recommend.

I tho this game is pretty cool along with with the armed reality that it has. Granted some of the maps for a little too big. For my home but with the new updates, it does come with little less requirements, which is a really good move on their end. I look forward to seeing more updates from this game.

reviews.review_title: Cool mixed reality game black ops game!
reviews.review_description: This game transforms your living space into a black ops mission zone filled with shoot outs and many side objective missions. Absolutely insane to witness an npc infiltrate by dropping into your room from the ceiling, one walk out from an elevator in the middle of your living room. Can work in small to large spaces. Highly recommend!
This is really good. Although I'm not sure why I dont seem to be able to replace a new magazine in the gun ? looked online but havn't found a solution. Apart from this its very good indeed

developer_response.body:
We're really glad to hear you're enjoying the game! As for reloading, there's 2 options. If you've selected "Manual Reload", then to place a new magazine, you 1) eject the old magazine by pressing the "A" or "X" button on your controller. 2) grab a new magazine from the 'ammo pouch' attached to your waist. 3) place the new magazine into the gun. If you selected "Auto Reload", then all you have to do is hold down the "A" or "X" button and the gun will just reload automatically. 

To switch between "Auto" and "Manual" reload, you can pause the game and go to "settings" -> "Gameplay". Let me know if you have any issues!

Cheers, 
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: it fun but hsrdcat fifdt
Best mixed reality game I’ve ever played. Very fun. I’d love to see more missions and enemy types in the future.

developer_response.body:
Hey Miniwhip. Thankyou so much for your review. We're so glad to hear you've been enjoying the game. We're certainly adding new content and so we hope the game will continue to entertain! 

Cheers,
EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire

reviews.review_description: It was a really fun use of mr once you get it working right it uses your space well and looks fantastic
Stuff like this is the future of gaming.

This is what an MR shooter should be.  having these agents in your home takes that immersion level to 1000.  hiding behind your couch, peaking around corners.  amazing job from the devs.  I have been a huge fan of the Espire line up, and this one is another home run.

Top notch mixed reality pass through game. The thing thats killing it for me is I dont have more control to tell the game to stay away from spawning game objectives and opening doors on certain walls. I have a big above the garage space but the game keeps driving me to the one area that has real life obstacles. Other MR games have options to help with this.

developer_response.body:
Hey BigSpock. Thanks for trying Espire MR, and for taking the time to leave a review. We're taking your feedback onboard and are working out how best to address it in a future update. We'll test more Mixed Reality games to get some inspiration on how to best give options to players. For now, I can suggest a workaround for you: 

If you 'edit' the Meta scan of your room, you can feel free to add virtual objects to your room in areas where you don't want our game to access. For example, Meta's room scan will give you an option to 'draw object' and you could draw a large box labelled 'other' that covers all of your real life obstacles. Our game will now no longer spawn any items in that location. 

Hope this helps you. If you have any further feedback, feel free to update your review, we'll get a notification to respond. 

Cheers, 

EspireMike from LODE, developers of Espire.

reviews.review_description: After a quick play this game is is really fun using good use of the space available in whichever room you’re playing in obviously the more space the better if you have played Espire before you will know what to expect if not for this price it’s well worth it to crawl move shoot and generally enjoy yourself get this
